content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES OF KROLL, LOEFFLER & WAGGONER
611 THIRTEENTH STREET • P.O.BOX 3489
MODESTO, CALIFORNIA 95353-3489

JAMES J. KROLL, JR.
MICHAEL G. LOEFFLER
CARL O. WAGGONER

TELEPHONE (209) 576-7777
FAX (209) 576-8437

DAVID CERVANTES

April 17, 1992

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95812

RE: Request for Written Advice

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

I am writing to request, pursuant to the provisions of Government Code Section 83114(b), formal written advice, regarding Martin G. Petersen, Stanislaus County Superintendent of School, duties under the Fair Political Practices Act. I have been requested to ask for formal written advice by Mr. Petersen.

Please consider the following:

The Stanislaus County Office of Education distributes a publication entitled "Education Newsletter." For your reference, I have enclosed a copy. This particular publication is delivered through County Schools' courier service to the various school sites throughout Stanislaus County. In excess of 5,000 copies are delivered in this manner. Copies are also available at the County office. In addition, pursuant to their written request, one hundred forty persons receive copies of this newsletter through the United States mail.

The County office would like to include in this publication an occasional comment by, photographs of, and articles by the County Superintendent, an elected public official. What has raised this issue is the desire of the County staff to have included in the next issue two photographs, copies of which I have enclosed. The text which accompanies the photographs contains a quote by Mr. Petersen and the photo caption includes Mr. Petersen's name. The photograph relates to the County office participation in a gang prevention program and is of Stanislaus County Sheriff Weidman and Mr. Petersen. The second photograph relates to the receipt

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
April 17, 1992

of a federal grant.

I have reviewed this matter and believe that the newsletter falls under the exception found in part b of Regulation 1860, "inter-agency communication." We have received conflicting advice over the telephone on this matter from your office and we want to ensure that a violation does not occur.

We have taken the opportunity to review similar newsletters from other counties and have observed that it is a fairly common practice to include photographs, names, comments and other references to the County Superintendent.

Please let me hear from you regarding the foregoing as soon as possible. Thank you for your assistance in this matter.

Very truly yours,

Law Offices of
KROLL, LOEFFLER & WAGGONER

CARL WAGGONER

CW/mer

Enclosure

cc: Stanislaus County Office of Education
    Attn: Martin Petersen, Superintendent

The image consists of two black and white photographs:

1. The top photograph shows two men standing in front of a banner that reads, "It Takes Courage To Stand Alone." The banner also includes the words "GANG PREVENTION WEEK." Both men are dressed formally, one holding a folder or document.

2. The bottom photograph shows another pair of men, both dressed formally, with one handing over a certificate or award to the other. They appear to be smiling and are standing in front of a wooden background.

These images seem to be related to an event or campaign focused on gang prevention and courage, as indicated by the banner text.
